background.experience: mass-casualty handoffs and controlled medication custody

readers.thesis: Custody survives only when recovery works without the primary operator or primary machine.
observations: Prior runs repeatedly separate service convenience from sovereign authority.

background.experience: separation of powers and continuity-of-government planning
background.counter_bias: rejects nominal quorum under one controlling account

readers.thesis: The bootstrap boundary acts as a constitution: no provider or single person controls system resurrection.
observations: Threshold custody converts recovery into governed authority.
uncertainties: Physical custody devices and succession policy remain unresolved.

agreements: Automatic bootstrap convenience can create an external lifecycle dependency.
Quorum requires independent operators and failure domains.
disagreements: Reader A prioritizes timed recovery; Reader B prioritizes authority separation.
omissions_detected: Both initially omitted immediate revocation of the initialization administrator.
revisions_after_discussion: The gate binds administrator revocation and two-operator recovery drills.

notes_added: Treat bootstrap recovery as release-authority continuity.
Count operator control as a failure domain.

privacy.review: Contains only synthetic technical abstractions and explicit coverage limits.
